skill.name: openclawemailskill
skill.description: Guides agents through auditable, provider-neutral email campaign planning, QA, migration, and approval-gated operations.
skill.summary: Create inspectable email campaign playbooks with clear evidence, QA checks, and approval gates.
skill.author: Sequenzy

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography and system reconnaissance patterns, but review found documentation-only text. No commands, scripts, credential access, network calls, or prompt injection attempts were found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged lines describe open email operations, audit trails, portable interpretations, and approval gates. No weak cryptographic algorithm or cryptographic operation appears in context.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed text contains ordinary email operations language, not hashes, ciphers, or crypto API names. The finding is strongly supported as a scanner keyword collision.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged line is a documentation heading about cases where the skill should not be used. It does not request host inspection or environment discovery.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is only the heading 'Avoid Using It For' within user guidance. No reconnaissance command, file enumeration, or system probing instruction is present.

content.value_statement: Email campaign work can hide risky assumptions and unclear approval boundaries. This skill creates inspectable playbooks, QA checks, and handoffs for agent-assisted email operations.
actual_capabilities: Classifies email work as inspect, plan, draft, migrate, QA, or approved execution.
Turns provider-specific email details into portable concepts and review steps.
Creates reversible playbooks before suggesting commands, API calls, or platform actions.
Separates evidence from recommendations for easier human review.
Adds explicit approval gates for sends, imports, DNS edits, and suppression changes.
Produces audit trails, migration checklists, review packets, and safe execution runbooks.
limitations: It does not connect to email service providers or send email.
It depends on the artifacts and context provided by the user.
It cannot verify live account settings without user-supplied evidence.
It requires human approval before high-risk operational changes.

use_cases.title: Audit a Campaign Handoff
use_cases.description: Review campaign artifacts and create an evidence-based handoff with risks, assumptions, and approval needs.

use_cases.title: Plan a Provider Migration
use_cases.description: Compare source and target provider concepts before moving lists, templates, automations, or metrics.

use_cases.title: Prepare Safe Execution Steps
use_cases.description: Convert a requested email operation into reversible steps with QA checks and explicit approval gates.

prompt_templates.title: Review My Email Task
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to classify my email task and list the artifacts needed for a safe review.

prompt_templates.title: Create a QA Checklist
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a provider-neutral QA checklist for this campaign using the files and notes I provide.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Migration
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare these provider exports and draft a migration playbook with assumptions, gaps, rollback notes, and approval gates.

prompt_templates.title: Build an Execution Runbook
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn this email operation into an auditable runbook that separates evidence, recommendations, commands, risks, and required human approvals.

output_examples.input: Review this planned contact import before execution.
output: Risk level: Medium, because the import affects audience membership.
Required evidence: source export, field map, suppression list, and approval owner.
Approval gate: Do not import until the owner confirms the preview counts.

output_examples.input: Help migrate a welcome series from one provider to another.
output: Source concepts: audience, trigger, delay, template, suppression, and reporting events.
Target checks: field parity, automation timing, unsubscribe behavior, and event tracking.
Rollback note: pause the target journey before restoring source automation traffic.

output_examples.input: Audit a campaign brief for hidden assumptions.
output: Evidence found: audience description, template notes, and send window.
Missing evidence: suppression source, approval owner, and rollback plan.
Recommendation: request missing artifacts before approving production work.

best_practices: Provide exports, screenshots, briefs, and platform names before asking for operational recommendations.
Keep live sends, imports, DNS edits, and suppression changes behind explicit approval.
Ask for evidence, recommendation, risk, and rollback sections in every runbook.
anti_patterns: Do not use it for generic email advice without campaign context.
Do not treat proposed commands or platform steps as approval to execute.
Do not assume provider features, fields, or automation behavior are equivalent.

faq.question: Does this skill send email campaigns?
faq.answer: No. It prepares review materials and runbooks. A human must approve and execute live sends.

faq.question: Can it work across different email providers?
faq.answer: Yes. It converts provider-specific terms into portable concepts for comparison and migration planning.

faq.question: What inputs are most useful?
faq.answer: Useful inputs include briefs, exports, template files, screenshots, logs, field maps, and prior campaign notes.

faq.question: How does it reduce operational risk?
faq.answer: It makes assumptions visible, separates evidence from recommendations, and adds approval gates for risky actions.

faq.question: Is this skill useful for Claude and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows that need auditable email operations guidance.

faq.question: Can it replace a compliance review?
faq.answer: No. It supports operational review, but legal, privacy, and compliance decisions need qualified human review.
